TSLA: Samsung quadruples monthly DRAM supply to Tesla- report. Samsung Electronics has quadrupled its monthly supply of 8GB GDDR6 DRAM to Tesla Inc. in April, responding to increased memory demand from the electric vehicle maker for its in-vehicle infotainment and autonomous driving systems. This surge comes amid tightening global memory chip supplies and Samsung's plans to produce advanced AI chips for Tesla at its Texas foundry in late 2026.

NIO Gives Its Model Y Nemesis LiDAR Vision — Wallet-Friendly Price Tag Unchanged. Nio Inc. has launched an updated version of its Onvo L90 SUV, its mass-market offering, which now includes a LiDAR sensor for enhanced assisted driving capabilities without an increase in its wallet-friendly price of RMB 265,800. This move by Nio contrasts with Tesla CEO Elon Musk's stance against LiDAR in favor of camera-only vision for assisted driving. The Onvo L90 continues to be priced significantly lower than Tesla's Model Y L in China, and Nio is expanding its Onvo sub-brand with a new five-seat SUV, the L80, slated for release in Q2.

YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF declares $0.0879 dividend. The YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY) has declared a cash dividend of $0.0879 per share. Shareholders on record as of April 24, 2026, will receive this dividend, which is payable on April 26, 2026. The ex-dividend date for this payout is April 24, 2026.

Morgan Stanley resets Intel stock price target ahead of earnings. Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for Intel (INTC) stock to $56 from $41, maintaining an equal-weight (hold) rating, ahead of its Q1 2026 earnings report. This adjustment comes after Intel's stock surged 78% year-to-date, driven by key strategic deals with Apollo, Tesla/SpaceX, and Google Cloud, which have fueled optimism despite a previous dip after disappointing Q4 earnings guidance. The analysts acknowledge significant upside potential from foundry partnerships and regaining market share, while also flagging risks from AMD competition and potential inefficiencies in its foundry business.

GM Gave $40M Package to Ex-Tesla, Aurora Exec as Product Chief, Filing Shows. General Motors offered a $40 million compensation package to recruit Sterling Anderson, formerly of Tesla and Aurora Innovation, as its Chief Product Officer. The package includes $16 million received in 2025 and an additional $24 million tied to performance targets and continued employment through 2027. This significant hiring and compensation package underscores GM's commitment to advancing its software and product development capabilities in the competitive EV market.

As Lam Research Joins the Tesla Terafab, Should You Buy, Sell, or Hold LRCX Stock?. Lam Research (LRCX) stock has seen significant growth, up over 318% in the last 52 weeks, driven by the booming semiconductor industry and recent news of potential collaboration with Elon Musk's Terafab project. This partnership, aimed at advanced 2-nanometer chip production, could be a major growth accelerator for Lam Research, which is a leading supplier of wafer fabrication equipment. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating for LRCX, forecasting substantial earnings growth and continued value creation in the expanding WFE market.

Asset Allocation Summit: Opportunities in Direxion’s Leveraged-Inverse Suite. During VettaFi's Asset Allocation Summit, Direxion's Edward Egilinsky discussed the growing opportunities in their leveraged-inverse ETF suite, especially given recent market volatility. He emphasized the critical importance of investor education due to the complex, derivatives-based nature of these products, which have been pioneered by Direxion since 2008. The summit highlighted both popular single-stock leveraged/inverse ETFs for companies like Tesla, Nvidia, and Micron, and also products for long-term investors like the Direxion NASDAQ-100 Equal Weighted Index ETF (QQQE).
TGT: St. Louis region's biggest Target store gets opening date. The largest Target store in the St. Louis region, located at 8647 Olive Blvd. in University City, is set to open on Sunday, May 17. The 149,000-square-foot store will be the 21st Target in the region and one of six new Target stores opening nationwide, featuring a two-story parking garage and a design suited for same-day delivery and extensive merchandise. Target Corp. (NYSE:TGT) also has plans for another store in Farmington, Missouri.

Target to Transform Community Spaces in 13 Cities Through Bullseye Builds. Target Corp. is expanding its Bullseye Builds with Community Program to 13 U.S. cities this year, backed by a $1 million investment. The initiative, which launched in 2024, transforms community spaces through design-driven projects like garden restorations and facility improvements, with the help of Target team member volunteers. Projects will kick off in May in Denver and Las Vegas, focusing on student wellness and combating food insecurity, respectively.
SBUX: Starbucks CEO, international Brady Brewer sells $58,800 in stock By Investing.com. Brady Brewer, Starbucks' CEO, International, sold 588 shares of company stock for $58,800 on April 17, 2026. The transaction was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. This sale comes as InvestingPro analysis suggests Starbucks appears overvalued, despite a history of 16 consecutive years of dividend increases.

Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) exec sells 588 shares under 10b5-1 plan. Starbucks' CEO International, Brady Brewer, sold 588 shares of company common stock at $100.00 per share on April 17, 2026, totaling $58,800. This transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on December 3, 2025. Following the sale, Brewer retains 83,787.502 shares of Starbucks common stock.

Starbucks CEO to Nashville: 'We're here … because we share a vision'. Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol visited Nashville to formally announce the opening of a new office that will create 2,000 jobs. Niccol explained the reasons behind the global coffee giant's decision to expand its presence in Music City. The move is part of Starbucks' efforts to spark a turnaround for the company.

Starbucks wins as court vacates NLRB unfair labor practice ruling. A federal appeals court has sided with Starbucks, ruling that the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) used an incorrect standard when penalizing the company for subpoenaing pro-union workers. The Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals vacated an NLRB order that found Starbucks committed an unfair labor practice by seeking information on union activities, forcing the Board to re-evaluate the case under a "totality of the circumstances" standard. This decision means the Board must use a more context-driven approach to determine if Starbucks' actions were coercive, rather than the previous discovery-related standard.

What to know about Starbucks’ Nashville job search. Starbucks has initiated its recruitment drive for 2,000 jobs at its new "eastern U.S. headquarters" in downtown Nashville, located in the Peabody Union development. The company seeks local talent for the majority of these six-figure positions. The article highlights the skills Starbucks is looking for, the expected origin of its Nashville-based employees, and salary information.

Starbucks aims for 2,000 jobs at its 'eastern headquarters' in Nashville. Starbucks officially announced details of its Nashville office expansion on April 21, with executives appearing publicly in Music City for the first time. The company plans to create 2,000 jobs at what it refers to as its "eastern headquarters" in Nashville, though it did not specify a timeline. This move expands Starbucks' corporate presence outside of its Seattle base.

Starbucks Corp. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors. Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) shares fell 1.16% on Tuesday, closing at $97.80, as the broader stock market also experienced a downturn. The S&P 500 Index dropped 0.63% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.59%. This marks the second consecutive day of losses for Starbucks stock, which underperformed its competitors.

Starbucks to open new corporate office in Nashville. Starbucks plans to open a new corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee, investing $100 million and creating up to 2,000 jobs over the next five years. While Seattle remains its global headquarters, this move supports a significant U.S. expansion strategy, including opening 5,000 new cafes. The company aims to address underserved regions and compete with rising coffee chains.

Starbucks to invest $100 million to set up Southeast corporate office in Nashville. Starbucks announced plans to invest $100 million in a new corporate office in Nashville, Tennessee. This new office will support the coffee chain's expansion in the southeastern United States and will complement its existing headquarters in Seattle. The company expects to create 2,000 new support jobs in Nashville over the next five years, including new roles and conversions of contract positions to full-time staff.

Starbucks earnings set to reflect stabilizing US trends offset by mixed international outlook. Starbucks is preparing to release its fiscal second-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating stabilizing US demand but mixed international performance, particularly in China. Jefferies expects in-line results with modest US improvement, while UBS projects stronger North America sales due to menu innovation and operational enhancements. Both firms acknowledge that much of the near-term recovery is already priced into the stock.
DIS: The Seven enters multi-year co-development deal with The Walt Disney Company - Señal News. The Seven has announced a multi-year co-development partnership with The Walt Disney Company to create high-quality Japanese live-action original content for global streaming distribution on Disney+. This historic collaboration marks Disney's first ongoing content development partnership with a Japanese production company for global streaming. The initiative aims to elevate Japanese stories into global IPs by combining The Seven’s creative and production capabilities with Disney's storytelling expertise.

THE SEVEN Enters Multi-Year Co-Development Deal with The Walt Disney Company (Japan) to Develop Live-Action Drama Series. THE SEVEN, Inc. has announced a multi-year co-development partnership with The Walt Disney Company (Japan) Ltd. to create high-quality Japanese live-action drama series for global distribution on Disney+. This collaboration marks Disney's first ongoing content development partnership with a Japanese production company for worldwide streaming. The initiative aims to elevate Japanese storytelling into global intellectual properties, with both companies working together from the initial planning stages to develop compelling projects.

Walt Disney Co. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day. Despite a general market downturn, Walt Disney Co. shares experienced a 1.89% dip to $104.29 on Tuesday, snapping a six-day winning streak. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw declines on what was a challenging trading day. This article highlights Disney's performance relative to the broader market, noting its slight fall amidst wider losses.
NKE: Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its next chief executive officer, effective September 8th. This decision comes after months of criticism regarding the company's management and performance, including pressure from founder Chip Wilson. O'Neill's extensive experience at Nike and other brands is expected to help Lululemon strengthen its brand relevance and accelerate growth amid increasing competition.

lululemon Names Proven Brand Builder Heidi O’Neill as Chief Executive Officer. lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ:LULU) has announced the appointment of Heidi O’Neill as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective September 8, 2026. O'Neill brings over three decades of experience, including a significant tenure at Nike, Inc., where she played a key role in its growth. She is tasked with accelerating product breakthroughs, deepening brand relevance, and unlocking global market growth for lululemon.

Dick’s Sporting Goods EVP sells $925k in stock. Julie Lodge-Jarrett, EVP at DICK’S Sporting Goods, sold over $925,000 in company stock on April 17, 2026, while also acquiring shares through stock option exercises. The transactions were reported to the SEC on April 21, 2026. This comes as Dick's Sporting Goods receives positive analyst ratings and strong sales reports for Nike running shoes, even as Goldman Sachs adjusts consumer spending forecasts.
